codexCorrections.reason: Verified all Y545 Additional Pure Mathematics requirements are present. Where the fixed 812-node ontology has no precise node, the candidate records an explicit unmappedReason; these are transparent ontology-granularity limitations, not omitted official points or invalid mappings.

issues: Broad argument-construction statement mapped to the closest proof/reasoning nodes; no single canonical node captures the full overarching theme scope.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping; no change required.

issues: Very broad statement about mathematical language and syntax maps to a single precise-use-of-symbols node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable broad mapping; no change required.

issues: Set-theory language and symbols are used in several specification areas; mapped to the generic set-language-and-notation node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable broad mapping; no change required.

issues: Problem-solving interpretation in context mapped to the modelling output node as the closest contextual-interpretation node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable broad mapping; no change required.

issues: Correctly left unmapped: the official specification explicitly states this overarching theme is not applicable to A Level Further Mathematics.
Correctly left unmapped: the official specification explicitly states this overarching theme is not applicable to A Level Further Mathematics.
Broad problem-solving cycle statement mapped to the general problem-solving-skills node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable broad mapping; no change required.

issues: Broad model-refinement and evaluation statement mapped to the general modelling-cycle node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable broad mapping; no change required.

issues: Canonical node catalog lacks a dedicated proof-by-induction node; mapped to general proof-by-deduction as the closest available partial fit.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping given canonical node availability; no change required.

issues: Canonical node catalog lacks a dedicated proof-by-induction node; mapped to general proof-by-deduction as the closest available partial fit.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping given canonical node availability; no change required.

issues: Terminology-only point covering multiple concepts (real/imaginary parts, conjugate, modulus, argument) mapped to several partial nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable distributed mapping; no change required.

issues: Point covers both cartesian and modulus-argument forms; mapped to relevant partial nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping; no change required.

issues: Notation point for conjugate, real/imaginary parts, modulus and argument maps to the same concept nodes as 4.02a.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable distributed mapping; no change required.

issues: Arithmetic in both cartesian and modulus-argument forms requires multiple partial nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping; no change required.

issues: Square roots are a special case of nth roots; the canonical nth-roots node is broader than the point.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable narrower-to-broader mapping; no change required.

issues: Point combines conjugate-pair and factor-theorem ideas across two nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable partial mapping; no change required.

issues: Selected nodes cover conjugate and Argand diagram aspects but do not fully capture the geometry of addition/subtraction of complex numbers.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add a dedicated complex-number addition/subtraction geometry node if the catalog is expanded.

issues: Set-notation aspect relies on the generic NUM-SYS-SET-LANG node because no loci-specific set-notation node exists.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable as-is; consider a more specific node if available.

issues: Multiple nodes are needed to cover theorem, multiple-angle formulae and series sums; no single node combines all three aspects.
Several terminology items (e.g. transpose, rectangular, m by n) are covered only implicitly by the broader matrix operations/determinants nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add finer matrix-terminology nodes if the catalog expands.

issues: Associative/commutative properties are a sub-topic of matrix multiplication rather than the whole node.
Mapped to the general determinants node; this point is restricted to 2x2 determinants.
Geometric interpretation of 2x2 determinant as area scale factor and orientation is not explicitly represented by the general determinants node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add a matrix-transformation-scale-factor node if the catalog is expanded.

issues: Mapped to the general determinants node; this point is restricted to 3x3 determinants.
Geometric interpretation of 3x3 determinant as volume scale factor is not explicitly represented by the general determinants node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add a matrix-transformation-scale-factor node if the catalog is expanded.

issues: Determinant-of-a-product property is a specific property not explicitly captured by the general determinants node.
Mapped to the general inverse-matrices node; this point is restricted to 2x2 inverses.
Mapped to the general inverse-matrices node; this point is restricted to 3x3 inverses.
Connection between inverse matrices and inverse transformations spans both inverse-matrix and transformation nodes.
Solving simultaneous equations by inverse matrix is only partially covered by the general inverse-matrices node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add a matrix-equations node if the catalog is expanded.

issues: No canonical node in the supplied catalog covers the classification of linear simultaneous equations as consistent (infinite solutions) or inconsistent (no solution).
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for linear-system consistency or place under an appropriate algebra/simultaneous-equations node.

issues: Interpretation of three-plane geometry is spread across plane and singularity nodes; no single node captures the three-planes classification.
Mapped only to the scalar-product node; the canonical catalog has no dedicated node for angles between planes or between a line and a plane.
pointReviews.recommendation: Accept with note; consider adding a dedicated node for plane/line-plane angles if the catalog is extended.

issues: The canonical distance node is broader than the specific parallel-line and skew-line distance requirements.
The canonical distance node is broader than the specific point-to-line distance requirement.
The canonical distance node is broader than the specific point-to-plane distance requirement.
Partial fit because two separate root/coefficient nodes are used; the quartic scope is covered only in combination.
Partial fit: irreducible-quadratic partial fractions and applications are covered, but no single node captures improper-rational-function partial fractions explicitly.
Broader mapping because the single point spans both differentiation and integration of hyperbolic functions, requiring two canonical nodes.
The inverse-hyperbolic node is broader than the specific logarithmic-form requirement.

The inverse-hyperbolic-derivative part is mapped to CALC-HYP-HYP-INVH, which primarily covers definitions/log forms rather than derivatives; the canonical catalog lacks a dedicated node for derivatives of inverse hyperbolic functions.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or use a dedicated inverse-hyperbolic-derivatives node if available; otherwise retain with a weak-fit note.

issues: Broader mapping because the point covers both inverse-trigonometric and inverse-hyperbolic substitutions, requiring two canonical nodes.

pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; general/particular solution concept is covered by the selected first-order nodes and second-order CFPI node.
Retain mapping; coupled systems and elimination are reasonably represented.
Retain mapping; the permutation/combination nodes cover the counting tools used in the probability context.

issues: The supplied canonical catalog contains no node for the discrete uniform distribution, so this specification point is left unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for the discrete uniform distribution (including notation U{1,n}, probabilities, mean and variance) and map this point to it.
Retain mapping; Poisson modelling assumptions fit within the Poisson-model node.

issues: The additive closure property of independent Poisson variables is mapped to PROB-DIST-POIS-MODE, which is broader and does not specifically represent sums of Poisson variables.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for properties of sums of independent random variables (or specifically Poisson additive closure) and remap.

issues: The specification point explicitly mentions continuous uniform and exponential distributions and cumulative distribution functions; the supplied catalog lacks nodes for these, so the mapping is incomplete.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add canonical nodes for continuous uniform distribution, exponential distribution and cumulative distribution functions, then extend the mapping.

issues: The point requires both mean and variance from a pdf; the catalog provides a node for the mean of a continuous random variable but not for variance, so the mapping is partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for the variance of a continuous random variable and include it in the mapping.

issues: General expectation E(g(X)) is mapped to PROB-DIST-CRV-MEAN, which represents only the special case g(X)=X; a more specific node for general expectations is absent.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for general expectation of a function of a continuous random variable and remap.

issues: The point concerns cumulative distribution functions, but no cdf node exists in the catalog; it is mapped to PROB-DIST-CRV-PDF as the closest available node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for cumulative distribution functions and remap.

issues: The point covers the pdf-cdf relationship and evaluation of median, quartiles and percentiles; the catalog lacks dedicated nodes, so it is mapped weakly to PROB-DIST-CRV-PDF.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add canonical nodes for cumulative distribution functions and percentiles of continuous random variables, then remap.

issues: Canonical node PROB-DIST-CRV-PDF covers probability density functions, not cumulative distribution functions of related variables/transformations.
No exact canonical node for transformations of continuous random variables exists in the supplied catalog.
pointReviews.recommendation: Map to a transformation-of-continuous-random-variables node if available, or retain a broader continuous-random-variables node with a partial relation.

issues: Spec point applies to linear combinations of any random variables (including continuous); mapped node PROB-DRVD-DRVB-LINX is restricted to linear transformations of discrete random variables.
pointReviews.recommendation: Use a general linear-combinations/expectation-algebra node, or add a note that the mapping is only partial.

issues: Spec point is specifically about linear combinations of normal random variables; PROB-DIST-NORM-MODE is a general normal-model node and does not explicitly cover additive/normal-preserving results.
pointReviews.recommendation: Map to a dedicated linear-combinations-of-normal-variables node, or mark the relation as partial.

issues: Confidence intervals for a population mean using a normal distribution are not captured by the broad STAT-EST-ESTI node; no normal-confidence-interval node is supplied in the catalog.
pointReviews.recommendation: Introduce or map to a normal-distribution confidence-interval node and mark the relation partial.

issues: Normal approximations and continuity corrections for large-sample Wilcoxon tests are not explicitly named in STAT-INF2-NONP-WSR/WRS nodes, though these nodes cover the tests themselves.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a note that the mapping covers the test but the normal-approximation aspect is only implicit.

issues: Mapped to the broad STAT-CORR-REG category node; the specific distinction between independent and dependent variables is not represented by a dedicated node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain the category mapping or create a regression-variables node, noting the narrower scope.

issues: Mapped to the root MECH node because the supplied catalog contains no dimensional-analysis node; the mapping loses the specific content of finding dimensions and using dimensional analysis.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dimensional-analysis canonical node, or map to the closest mechanics sub-domain and clearly mark the relation as narrower.

issues: Mapped only to the broad MECH root node because the canonical catalog contains no dedicated dimensional-analysis node; semantic fit is weak.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a dedicated dimensional-analysis canonical node and remap; until then, retain the Mechanics root mapping with a weak-fit flag.

issues: Mapped only to the broad MECH root node because the canonical catalog contains no dedicated dimensional-analysis node; semantic fit is weak.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a dedicated dimensional-analysis canonical node and remap; until then, retain the Mechanics root mapping with a weak-fit flag.

issues: Mapped only to the broad MECH root node because the canonical catalog contains no dedicated dimensional-analysis node; semantic fit is weak.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a dedicated dimensional-analysis canonical node and remap; until then, retain the Mechanics root mapping with a weak-fit flag.

issues: Mapped only to the broad MECH root node because the canonical catalog contains no dedicated dimensional-analysis node; semantic fit is weak.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a dedicated dimensional-analysis canonical node and remap; until then, retain the Mechanics root mapping with a weak-fit flag.
No action required; the dual-node mapping to power and scalar product reasonably captures the variable-force vector extension.
No action required; the narrower relation correctly reflects that the point is a 2D specialisation of the general conservation node.

issues: Relation marked 'partial' but the concept of impulse is fully embedded within the impulse-momentum principle node; 'narrower' or 'exact' would be more precise.
pointReviews.recommendation: Reconsider the relation as 'narrower' rather than 'partial', or document why the impulse concept is judged only partially covered by the impulse-momentum principle node.
No action required; the narrower relation correctly reflects that the point is a 2D specialisation of the general principle.
No action required; the narrower relation correctly reflects that constant/variable force formulations are specialisations of the general principle.
No action required; the narrower relation correctly reflects that the terminology is a sub-topic of the restitution node.

issues: Mapped to the generic MECH-FURT-CIRC node, but the canonical catalog contains a more specific node MECH-FURT-CIRC-ANGS for angular velocity/speed definitions.
pointReviews.recommendation: Re-map to MECH-FURT-CIRC-ANGS, or add a dedicated circular-motion definitions node if ANGS is not intended to cover this point.

issues: The canonical node MECH-FURT-CIRC-VCIR is specific to vertical circular motion, whereas the syllabus point concerns variable speed in a circle generally.
pointReviews.recommendation: Use a general variable-speed circular-motion node if one exists; otherwise retain VCIR as the closest available node and document the scope mismatch.

issues: Radial and tangential acceleration components are not represented; mapping only to the parent MECH-FURT-CIRC node is too coarse. The radial component could at least map to MECH-FURT-CIRC-RACC.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a tangential-acceleration sub-node or split the mapping so the radial part uses MECH-FURT-CIRC-RACC.

issues: No canonical node exists for classifying discrete problems as existence, construction, enumeration or optimisation problems.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a discrete problem-types / modelling classification node.

issues: NUM-SYS-SET-LANG covers basic set language and notation but does not represent partition counting or the partition-of-a-set examples in the syllabus.
The canonical catalog has no node for the pigeonhole principle.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a counting principles / pigeonhole-principle node.

issues: The inclusion-exclusion principle for two sets is not represented by a dedicated node; NUM-SYS-SET-VENN only partially overlaps.
Extension of inclusion-exclusion to more than two sets is not represented by a dedicated node; NUM-SYS-SET-VENN only partially overlaps.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add an inclusion-exclusion principle node that covers three or more sets.

issues: The canonical catalog has no node for derangements.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a derangements / restricted permutations node.

issues: Using a colouring argument to decide bipartiteness is not represented by a dedicated node; DISC-GRAPH-GRAPH-TERM is too general.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a bipartite graphs / graph-colouring node, or map under a bipartite-graphs sub-node if introduced.

issues: Mapped to the route inspection/travelling salesperson node; Eulerian/semi-Eulerian graph determination is a related prerequisite, so the fit is partial rather than exact.
pointReviews.recommendation: Accept as a partial fit; add a dedicated Eulerian-graph node if the catalogue is extended.

issues: Hamiltonian path/cycle definitions are mapped to the route inspection/travelling salesperson node; this is related but only a partial fit.
pointReviews.recommendation: Accept as a partial fit; consider a dedicated Hamiltonian-graph node for an exact mapping.

issues: Ore's theorem is a sufficiency condition for Hamiltonian graphs; the travelling salesperson/route inspection node does not capture theorem-specific reasoning.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep as a weak fit; a dedicated Hamiltonian graph-theory node is needed for an exact mapping.

issues: Graph isomorphism is a structural property broader than general graph terminology; no dedicated isomorphism node exists in the catalogue.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep the broad mapping pending addition of a graph-isomorphism/congruence node.

issues: Digraph concepts map to the general graph terminology node; no dedicated directed-graph node exists.
Planarity, subdivision and contraction are graph properties that go beyond general terminology; no dedicated planar-graph node exists.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep the broad mapping; consider a dedicated planar-graph node.

issues: Euler's formula for planar graphs maps to the general graph terminology node; no dedicated planar-graph node exists.
Kuratowski's theorem is a planarity criterion; the general graph terminology node cannot represent it exactly.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep the broad mapping; consider a dedicated planarity/theorem node.

issues: Thickness is a specialised planar-graph property; no dedicated node exists.
Networks as weighted graphs and their use in modelling map to a general modelling node and a terminology node; these are broader than the specific content.
Adjacency and weighted-matrix representation maps to the general graph terminology node; acceptable but broader than the specific skill.
Modelling and solving problems with graphs/networks maps to generic modelling and general graphs/networks nodes; the fit is partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Accept the mapping; consider a dedicated network-modelling node.

issues: Definition and properties of algorithms map to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Uses and limitations of algorithms map to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Tracing and interpreting algorithms map to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Order of an algorithm and scaling run-times map to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Comparing algorithm efficiency maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Worst-case time complexity maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Big-O notation for polynomial orders maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Best, worst and typical case analysis maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
Hierarchy of orders including logarithmic and exponential orders maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; broader than the specific statement.
One-dimensional packing heuristics map to the general algorithms and optimisation node; no dedicated packing node exists.
Extension to 2D/3D packing and knapsack problems maps to the general algorithms and optimisation node; no dedicated packing node exists.
Primary fit to DISC-ALGO-ALGO is reasonable, but REAS-MODL-MODL is tangential; the statement is about choosing/adapting a network algorithm, not the full modelling cycle.
A more precise secondary node would be DISC-GRAPH-GRAPH (Graphs and Networks).
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep DISC-ALGO-ALGO and replace REAS-MODL-MODL with DISC-GRAPH-GRAPH if a second node is required.

issues: Dual-node mapping is appropriate because the statement explicitly links mixed strategies to linear programming.
Section overview covers recurrence relations, series, induction, Fibonacci-like sequences and modelling, but ALGF-SEQ-SEQU only captures generic sequences.
The catalog lacks nodes for recurrence relations and their solution.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain ALGF-SEQ-SEQU as the closest umbrella node; add canonical nodes for recurrence relations if detailed mapping is required.

issues: NUM-SYS is too high-level: the overview includes modular arithmetic, congruences and Fermat’s little theorem, which are not represented in the catalog.
Only prime numbers and set-notation have plausible sub-nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Map to more specific existing nodes (e.g. NUM-SYS-TYPE-PRIM, NUM-SYS-SET-LANG) where applicable and add number-theory/modular-arithmetic nodes to the catalog.

issues: The catalog contains no group-theory nodes, so no semantic fit can be established for group axioms, cyclic groups, Lagrange’s theorem or isomorphism.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as unmapped until group-theory nodes are added to the canonical catalog.

issues: The overview covers functions of two variables, contours/sections, partial derivatives, tangent planes and Hessian classification, but only CALC-DIFF-APPL-STAT (stationary points) is mapped.
The catalog lacks nodes for partial differentiation, 3-D surfaces and tangent planes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain CALC-DIFF-APPL-STAT for the stationary-point aspect; add multivariable calculus/surface nodes to the catalog for full coverage.

issues: Mapped only to the generic Sequences node; recurrence relations and closed-form position-to-term formulae are not represented.
Relation 'partial' is consistent but indicates non-exact coverage.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or map to a dedicated recurrence-relations / sequence-representation node, or retain as acceptable with a coverage note.

issues: Mapped to the generic Proof Methods node; proof by induction for sequences and series is not represented by a dedicated node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add an induction-specific node, or keep the generic proof mapping with an explicit coverage gap note.

issues: Mapped to the generic Sequences node; the specific vocabulary (periodic, convergent, divergent, oscillating, monotonic) and sequence-behaviour concepts are not captured.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated node for sequence behaviour / convergence concepts, or mark the mapping as weak.

issues: Mapped to the generic Sequences node; limits of the nth term and steady-states are not represented.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated node for sequence limits / steady-state behaviour, or mark the mapping as weak.

issues: The point covers both arc lengths and surface areas of revolution; separate canonical nodes cover each topic, so the mapping is broader.

pointReviews.recommendation: Treat as a supporting requirement rather than a content node mapping.

issues: Appendix 5d is a broad recall/use list of formulae and identities; it is not a discrete section-2 content statement.
pointReviews.recommendation: Treat as a supporting requirement rather than a content node mapping.

issues: This point records formulae provided in assessments; it is not a discrete section-2 content statement.

issues: Discrete uniform distribution is not represented in the canonical node catalogue, so the mapping omits it.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for the discrete uniform distribution and include it in the mapping.

issues: Continuous uniform and exponential distributions are not represented in the canonical node catalogue; the mapping also omits variance, c.d.f. and other continuous-distribution features.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add canonical nodes for continuous uniform and exponential distributions, c.d.f. and variance, and refine the mapping.

issues: The review note incorrectly describes the point as the chi-squared test statistic; the source reference is the non-parametric tests section (Wilcoxon approximations).
pointReviews.recommendation: Correct the review note to describe non-parametric test formulae.

issues: The cited source is a formulae-booklet page (p.115) rather than the corresponding content statement 5.07f.
The point covers only the large-sample normal approximations, so the mapping to the whole Wilcoxon signed-rank/rank-sum test nodes is only partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain the nodes but cite OCR Ref. 5.07f as the primary source, and keep relation as partial.

issues: Source reference is the formulae-booklet spread (pp.115-116) rather than the content statements 5.08/5.09, but the content is fully supported.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally add a content-statement citation.

issues: The source is a formulae-booklet page (p.121), not a content statement.
MECH-FURT-PROJ-HV is projectile-specific and is not an exact match for the general vector constant-acceleration kinematics on p.121.
pointReviews.recommendation: Split the projectile-specific aspect from the general constant-acceleration node, or clarify that the point is limited to projectile horizontal/vertical motion.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.121) rather than content statement 6.03i-l, but content is fully supported.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 6.03.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.121) rather than content statement 6.05.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 6.05.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.121) rather than content statement 6.04.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 6.04.

issues: Relation is marked 'exact' but no canonical node is supplied; these are inconsistent.
The supplied node catalog does not contain a node for the inclusion-exclusion counting principle.
pointReviews.recommendation: Map to the closest available node (e.g. NUM-SYS-SET-UNIN or PROB-COMB-COUNT) as 'acceptable' and flag the catalog gap, or request a new canonical node.

issues: Relation is marked 'exact' but no canonical node is supplied; these are inconsistent.
The supplied node catalog does not contain a node for asymptotic order hierarchy / Big O notation.
pointReviews.recommendation: Flag the catalog gap and, if necessary, map to DISC-ALGO-ALGO as 'weak' or leave unmapped.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.122) rather than content statement 7.03j/k.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 7.03.

issues: The simplex algorithm is a linear-programming method, not a network algorithm; mapping it under DISC-GRAPH-GRAPH-SHOR/MST is a weak fit.
The point bundles several distinct algorithms with different canonical homes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Split into separate points: network algorithms (Dijkstra/Prim/Kruskal/nearest-neighbour/route inspection) and simplex linear programming (DISC-ALGO-ALGO-LP).

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.125) rather than content statement 8.04a.
Paper eligibility is correctly inferred, not fixed.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 8.04a.

issues: The supplied node catalog has no node for the Hessian matrix or classification of stationary points of 3-D surfaces z = f(x,y).
Relation 'broader' is acceptable given the catalog gap, but nodeFit should remain unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Request a new canonical node for multivariable stationary-point classification / Hessian, and cite OCR Ref. 8.05e/f.

issues: The supplied node catalog has no node for tangent planes to 3-D surfaces using partial derivatives.
Relation 'broader' is acceptable given the catalog gap, but nodeFit should remain unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Request a new canonical node for tangent planes to surfaces, and cite OCR Ref. 8.05g.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.125) rather than content statement 8.06b.
Paper eligibility is correctly inferred, not fixed.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 8.06b.

issues: Source is a formulae-booklet page (p.125) rather than content statement 8.06b.
Paper eligibility is correctly inferred, not fixed.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change needed; ideally cite OCR Ref. 8.06b.

overallIssues.issue: The canonical node catalog does not contain a dedicated proof-by-induction node. Points 4.01a and 4.01b are therefore mapped to the general proof-by-deduction node as the closest available partial fit, which reduces mapping precision.

Several determinant-geometric-interpretation points (2x2 area scale factor/orientation and 3x3 volume scale factor) are mapped only to the general determinants node because no dedicated matrix-transformation-scale-factor node exists in the catalog.
Point 4.03s (classifying linear simultaneous equations as consistent/inconsistent) is genuinely unmapped because the supplied canonical catalog contains no node for linear-system consistency.

A number of points use broader or multiple nodes because the catalog lacks fine-grained nodes for specific properties (set notation in loci, determinant product, inverse-transformation link, matrix equation solving, three-plane geometry).

Inconsistent paperApplicability.kind within the same mandatory Pure Core section: 4.04c–4.04f are marked 'fixed' while later Pure Core points are marked 'eligible', although the official specification allocates the whole Pure Core to the mandatory papers Y540 and Y541.

Canonical node catalog lacks dedicated nodes for some Further Vectors and Further Calculus subtopics, leading to partial or weak fits: angles between planes/line-and-plane, and derivatives of inverse hyperbolic functions.

Inconsistent syllabusPointId prefix: points 4.10a-5.02h use 'OFFICIAL-OCR-H245-' while points 5.02i-5.03f use 'OFFICIAL-H245-'. A uniform identifier scheme should be adopted across all candidate points.

Several specification concepts are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes, resulting in unmapped or weak mappings: discrete uniform distribution, cumulative distribution function, continuous uniform and exponential distributions, variance of a continuous random variable, general expectation E(g(X)), and additive closure of independent Poisson variables.

Some candidate points use broader or partial node mappings because the canonical catalog is not granular enough for every specification statement; the resulting mappings are acceptable but could be refined if additional nodes are added.

Several Statistics points rely on overly broad or adjacent canonical nodes because the catalog lacks nodes for transformations of continuous random variables, general linear combinations of random variables, linear combinations of normal variables, and normal-distribution confidence intervals.
Normal approximations for large-sample Wilcoxon tests and the independent/dependent variable distinction are mapped to test or category nodes without explicit sub-topic coverage.
Dimensional analysis (OCR Ref. 6.01a) is mapped to the root Mechanics node because no dimensional-analysis node exists in the catalog.

Dimensional analysis statements (6.01b-e) are mapped only to the broad MECH root node because the canonical catalog lacks a dedicated dimensional-analysis node; the semantic fit is weak and should be flagged for node-catalog expansion.

Inconsistent paperApplicability.kind values are used for content that all belongs to the single Mechanics paper Y543 ('fixed' for 6.01-6.02f, then 'eligible' for 6.02g-6.04a). The official specification explicitly assigns the Mechanics optional content to Y543, so 'fixed' is supportable throughout.

Several circular-motion points are mapped to the generic MECH-FURT-CIRC node even though more specific canonical sub-nodes exist (e.g., MECH-FURT-CIRC-ANGS, MECH-FURT-CIRC-RACC, MECH-FURT-CIRC-HCIR, MECH-FURT-CIRC-VCIR), reducing the mapping granularity.

A cluster of discrete-mathematics enumeration and graph-theory topics are unmapped or weakly mapped because the canonical catalog lacks nodes for problem-type classification, the pigeonhole principle, derangements, inclusion-exclusion, and bipartite-colouring arguments.

The three distinct centre-of-mass methods (symmetry, composite/system, integration) all share the single MECH-FURT-RIGD-COM node because the catalog does not provide finer COM sub-nodes. The mapping is acceptable but not granular.

The canonical node catalogue lacks dedicated nodes for several discrete-mathematics topics (Eulerian/Hamiltonian graphs, planarity, digraphs, graph isomorphism, algorithmic complexity/order, and packing). Consequently many points are mapped to overly broad nodes with partial, broader or weak fit.

All source references correspond to the official H245 specification and every point is correctly allocated to the Discrete Mathematics paper Y544. No source-support or paper-allocation failures were detected.

Several Additional Pure Mathematics section-overview points map to high-level or missing canonical nodes because the catalog lacks coverage of recurrence relations, modular arithmetic/congruences, group theory, and multivariable calculus (surfaces, partial derivatives, tangent planes, Hessian).
7.04f includes a secondary canonical node (REAS-MODL-MODL) that is only tangentially related to the network-algorithm focus of the statement.

The canonical node catalogue lacks coverage for many Additional Pure Mathematics topics (recurrence relations, number bases, modular arithmetic, linear congruences, group theory, etc.), leaving 20 of the 30 supplied points unmapped.
Candidate relation is set to 'exact' for 20 points that have no canonical node mapping; this is inconsistent and should be 'unmapped' or omitted until a suitable node exists.

Mappings to generic nodes (Sequences, Proof Methods, Factors and multiples, Binomial expansions) are acceptable but do not capture the specific Additional Pure content; more granular nodes would improve coverage.

A substantial block of Additional Pure Mathematics content on group theory (cyclic groups, generators, small finite groups, Lagrange's theorem, group isomorphism and abstract groups) is not represented by canonical nodes.

A substantial block of Additional Pure Mathematics content on multivariable calculus and surface geometry (functions of several variables, sections and contours, partial derivatives, stationary points, Hessian matrix, tangent planes) is not represented by canonical nodes.
Statistics distribution mappings omit distributions that are absent from the canonical node catalogue (discrete uniform, continuous uniform and exponential distributions).
Review note for the non-parametric test point misidentifies the source content as a chi-squared formula.

Many points cite only formulae-booklet pages rather than the corresponding content statements. The formulae booklet confirms formulae but does not itself define assessment depth, relation scope, or paper mapping; the audit accepts the mappings because the qualification structure and content sections support them, but the evidence should include the relevant OCR Ref. (e.g. 5.07f, 5.08/5.09, 6.03-6.05, 7.01/7.03/7.04, 8.04-8.06).

The supplied canonical node catalog lacks nodes for four assessed topics: the inclusion-exclusion counting principle, the hierarchy of computational orders, the Hessian matrix/classification of 3-D surface stationary points, and tangent planes to 3-D surfaces. The candidate correctly flags the unmapped status, but in two cases the relation is inconsistently marked 'exact' while no node is supplied.

OFFICIAL-H245-Y544-DISC-NETWORK-ALGOS bundles the simplex algorithm with network algorithms; simplex is a linear-programming algorithm and is not a network algorithm, so the DISC-ALGO-ALGO-LP node fit is weak within this point.
OFFICIAL-H245-Y543-MECH-KINEMATICS maps general constant-acceleration vector kinematics to a projectile-specific node; the fit is only partial/acceptable.

Significant catalog gaps in Additional Pure Mathematics (Y545): canonical nodes are missing for number theory (bases, modular arithmetic, congruences, Fermat's little theorem, etc.), group theory (binary operations, Cayley tables, group axioms, subgroups, cyclic groups, Lagrange's theorem, isomorphism, etc.), recurrence relations (Fibonacci/Lucas numbers, first- and second-order linear recurrences, modelling), and multivariable calculus/surfaces (functions of several variables, sections/contours, partial derivatives, stationary points, Hessian matrix, tangent planes). Consequently many official points are represented only as unmapped or broad entries, limiting the granularity and usefulness of the index.

Inconsistent candidate ID prefix within the Statistics optional paper: entries from 5.02i onwards drop the 'OCR-' component (using 'OFFICIAL-H245-...' instead of 'OFFICIAL-OCR-H245-...'), while earlier Statistics entries and all other sections retain the full prefix. This inconsistency could impair automated matching or reporting.

Mechanics dimensional analysis points (6.01a-e) are mapped only to the generic 'MECH' node rather than to canonical nodes specific to dimensions, units, dimensional checking, or determining unknown indices. The specific content of each point is therefore not distinguished in the index.

Many Discrete Mathematics graph-theory and algorithm points are mapped to overly broad canonical nodes (e.g., 'DISC-GRAPH-GRAPH-TERM' or 'DISC-ALGO-ALGO') rather than to specific nodes for Eulerian/Hamiltonian graphs, planarity, isomorphism, digraphs, complexity classes, or packing. This reduces the discriminating power of the index for a large block of the optional Y544 content.

Cumulative distribution function points in Statistics (5.03e-g) are mapped to the probability density function node 'PROB-DIST-CRV-PDF' rather than to a distinct CDF node, potentially conflating two related but distinct concepts.

The Additional Pure Mathematics section is represented redundantly: section-level entries (8.01-8.06) and appendix-style entries (AP-*) overlap with the detailed OCR-ref entries (8.01a-8.06b). While this does not duplicate official point IDs, it creates parallel indexing for the same content and may complicate maintenance.
